business_ideas.idea: Revenue per employee metric: need 300-400K per employee for SaaS to be scalable
Multi-product expansion at 10K customers: second product must be bigger than first
Net revenue retention of 100%: expansion revenue must offset churn completely
International expansion: wait for 5% revenue cluster then invest in new markets
Founder-led pricing: underprice early products then hire VP of Sales to correct
Trade show business: SaaStr Annual charges 1K per attendee to break even
Raise one-time seed capital under 2M, avoid VC to maintain optionality for exits

predictions.text: Software companies reaching 300-400K revenue per employee become sustainable
Most public SaaS companies converging on 310-400K revenue per employee efficiency
Trade shows are terrible businesses unless you clear 15M revenue profitably
